subject.name: Stout rocks glass of neat bourbon on a charred oak stave-end

physical_properties.primary_element: stout rocks glass of deep mahogany-amber bourbon, neat, no ice; thick base catching warm refractions
physical_properties.secondary_element: worn American oak barrel stave-end serving as a coaster, exposed char layer visible at one edge
physical_properties.tertiary_element: tarnished brass bottle-hunter's keyring with three aged bottle medallions resting beside the glass

foreground.notes: positioned on the rule-of-thirds intersection so upper-left two-thirds remain clean for overlay text; bourbon glows mahogany-amber from internal back-lighting; thick faceted base
rough-cut barrel stave-end serving as a coaster, char layer crackle visible at the chamfered edge, signals authentic provenance
tarnished brass keyring with three aged bottle medallions (no readable text or logos), patina suggests years of cellar visits

midground.notes: partial out-of-focus stack of charred barrel staves cropped at frame edge, char layer catching warm rim light, supporting element only

background.notes: single dim Edison-bulb pendant hanging out of focus in the deep background, providing one warm practical light pool on the upper-left for text legibility
deep mahogany or walnut wainscoting with subtle Americana cabin tones, vertical grain, mostly in shadow
very subtle volumetric haze across the frame, catches the key light into soft god-rays without obscuring detail

surface.description: aged dark walnut tabletop with subtle ring-stain patina and a soft satin sheen, not glossy; reads as a well-loved tasting bench

background.negative_space_zone: upper_left_to_center — keep visually quiet for overlay text

atmosphere.mood: well-educated bourbon enthusiast, dusty-bottle pursuit, Pappy/Weller lore, rugged Americana but still prestigious and after-hours members-only

camera.focus_point: bourbon surface meniscus and glass rim
camera.composition_notes: rule of thirds — subject locked to lower-right intersection; upper-left two-thirds reserved for event title overlay

lighting.key_light_quality: warm tungsten ~2700K, soft falloff, motivated by the unseen Edison-bulb pendant; backlights the bourbon so the pour glows mahogany-amber from within
lighting.rim_light_notes: subtle warm rim on the rocks-glass rim and the charred edge of the stave to separate them from the background; faint rim on the brass keyring
lighting.practical_lights: distant out-of-focus warm bokeh points in deep background (suggesting cellar shelving / candles)

brand.palette_intent: warm gold and burgundy accents on near-black warm base, matching Lounge & Co. design tokens

controls.render_intent: photoreal, shot on medium-format, low ISO, fine grain
